[2026-04-14 15:47] Good catch on the ambiguity, Finn. Resolution:

**Use `docs/jira-gitflow-assessment-2026-04-14.md`** (i.e. `.claude/teams/framework-research/docs/jira-gitflow-assessment-2026-04-14.md`), same precedent as your uikit-dev harvest.

Reasoning: `topics/` is reserved for framework-research **design topic files** (T01–T08, the multi-team agent framework we're building). The Eesti Raudtee Jira/GitFlow pipeline is an **external system being assessed** — it's not a framework design topic and would not migrate into a topic file even at maturity. Creating `topics/jira-gitflow-pipeline.md` would mis-shelve it and pollute the topic namespace.

`docs/` is the right shelf for assessment digests of external systems and one-shot research artifacts. Your prompt's strict-letter list is a known gap I'll flag for Celes at next prompt-revision pass — the uikit-dev harvest already established the precedent and you're operating on confirmed precedent here, not improvising.

Proceed with `docs/jira-gitflow-assessment-2026-04-14.md`. ETA still short. Send me the path + 5-bullet summary when complete.

(*FR:team-lead*)

[2026-04-14 17:15] Finn — Phase 2 classification is **HOLDING**, not proceeding this session.

**Reason:** PO knows the Confluence author and will reach out directly to reconcile the two pages on the top divergences (Q1 `production` branch real or stale, Q2 `release/*` short- or long-lived, Q4 same project or not). Classifying the pattern/gotcha/decision candidates before reconciliation risks filing wrong-facts entries into the wiki — e.g. a "production branch is stale terminology" gotcha that turns out to be real.

**Your digest is the hand-off artifact.** PO is using `docs/jira-gitflow-assessment-2026-04-14.md` as the basis for the external conversation. Your work is not wasted — it's moving upstream instead of into the wiki this round.

**Phase 2 resumes** when PO returns with authoritative answers from the author. At that point I'll brief you for partial-or-full classification depending on which questions got resolved. No ETA; could be this session, could be next.

**Status update:**
- Phase 1 digest: DELIVERED and accepted. Thank you — the digest caught the divergence cleanly, including the Divergence 1 / Divergence 4 load-bearing finding and the 2025-02 vs 2026-04-13 versioning trail that made the hypothesis concrete. Strong work.
- Phase 2: HELD pending external reconciliation.
- Persist-coverage Fix/Design backlog: stays QUEUED (unchanged).
- Post-freeze Cal candidates: stay QUEUED (unchanged).
- Aalto 6 questions: stay QUEUED (unchanged).

**You're free to idle.** No new task assignment. Stand by; I'll wake you when PO returns with answers or when another task needs you.

(*FR:team-lead*)
summary: Hold Phase 2 — PO reconciling with author

text:
[2026-04-15 08:54] Finn — quick read-only lookup task, single round-trip.

PO wants the real identities behind the two accountIds in your digest's Provenance section. Two API calls, then idle again.

**Task:** For each of these Atlassian Cloud accountIds, fetch the user record via Jira REST API v3 user endpoint:

1. `712020:84a1b5ab-8627-4a1e-a50a-e4cc3167a890` (creator of both pages, sole author of Source 2)
2. `712020:da2c091f-fbc8-4a11-aff9-0c08f4f9713e` (v3 editor of Source 1)

**Endpoint:** `GET $ATLASSIAN_BASE_URL/rest/api/3/user?accountId=<accountId>` — same credentials you already used (`~/.claude/.env`: `ATLASSIAN_EMAIL`, `ATLASSIAN_API_TOKEN`).

**Privacy note:** `emailAddress` may be hidden by Atlassian Cloud privacy settings — return whatever the API gives you. The fields most likely available are `displayName`, `accountType`, `active`, and `avatarUrls`. `emailAddress` is a bonus.

**Reply format — single message, structured table:**

| accountId (last 8 chars) | displayName | emailAddress (if visible) | accountType | active |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `...` | ... | ... | ... | ... |
| `...` | ... | ... | ... | ... |

Plus a one-line note if either lookup returned a 404 or anything unexpected.

**Constraints (unchanged from session):** strict read-only, no Confluence edits, no git writes, no subagent spawning, no contact with Cal. Persist-coverage / uikit-dev Aalto / post-freeze Cal candidates / Phase 2 classification all stay queued. This is a side errand, not a Phase 2 wake.

Stand down to idle after delivering. Phase 2 hold remains in effect.

(*FR:team-lead*)
